_spawn_isolation_note: Loop body inline in main (Opus); Step-3 reviewer + HALT challenger spawned as independent subagents.
verdict: Good app, but not top-tier yet
verdict_explanation: search_boards continues to decompose: this loop extracts the pure filter decision into a tested _evaluate_filters, leaving the terminal rendering as the last big inlined block. The filter half of F-006 is resolved; the render half (which also subsumes the duplicated render blocks of F-005) is the next and final structural lever.

architecture_quality.proof: at loop-5 start search_boards still fuses filtering + date-scan + rendering; the filter extraction is this loop's fix (scored next loop)

state_management.proof: single-threaded; one writer per concern; immutable instance attrs

domain_modeling.proof: BoardResult + ParsedBoard name the records; sounds_info is a deliberate (id, title) 2-tuple — not worth a NamedTuple per the state/domain guardrail against forcing types

data_flow.proof: named-field contracts; residual: main re-filters results (~1590) duplicating search_boards' filter

framework_idioms.proof: idiomatic stdlib; defensive sanitization; HTTPS; no secrets

concurrency.proof: no async/threading — synchronous CLI, no shared-mutable hazard
concurrency.residual_blocking_10: time.sleep pacing is intentional server etiquette, not a determinism hazard
concurrency.residual_rationale_or_backlog_ref: Tool is deliberately synchronous; no concurrency model to make safer. Permanent design carve-out.

simplicity.proof: dead wrapper removed (commit bd81479); search_boards continues to shrink. Residual: ~678-line function still inlines the date-scan + two render sections, with duplicated render blocks (1191 vs 1368; 1281 vs 1393)

test_strategy.proof: 28 tests at loop-5 start; the 6 new filter tests are this loop's addition (scored next loop). Residual: filter eval was untested at loop start; rendering still untested

credibility.proof: two named domain records, parse test-backed; code remains honest with no fake-clean

strengths: Three behavior-preserving, independently-reviewed extractions so far (BoardResult, _parse_board_html, and this loop's filter evaluator) — a consistent, honest refactor cadence
The filter decision is now a pure function with full branch coverage including the date-only-when-basics-pass rule
Synchronous design + defensive sanitization remain real strengths

findings.title: search_boards still fuses filtering, the network date-scan and all terminal rendering (~690 lines)
findings.why_it_matters: Filter evaluation and result rendering are pure/near-pure but cannot be tested while inlined in the network loop.
findings.what_is_wrong: search_boards inlines the filter evaluation (addressed this loop), the best-effort date-scan reduction, and two large rendering sections (per-board summary + final results render).
findings.leverage_impact: Maintainers still hold the render logic in the function scope to change output.
findings.locality_impact: A formatting change still risks the rest of the function via shared locals.
findings.why_weakens_submission: Rendering is testable logic kept unreachable by fusion with I/O; this caps architecture, simplicity and test strategy until extracted.
findings.minimal_correction_path: This loop: extract a pure _evaluate_filters(...) returning (meets, failures), keeping the skipped_buckets attribution at the call site. Next loop: extract pure render/format helpers (BoardResult -> str), which also folds the duplicated blocks in F-005. No class hierarchy.

findings.title: Date-display and skipped-breakdown rendering duplicated near-verbatim
findings.why_it_matters: Two copies of the same presentation logic must be kept in sync by hand; they will drift.
findings.what_is_wrong: The approx-updated date block and the skipped-buckets breakdown block each appear twice with near-identical bodies.
findings.leverage_impact: A formatting change must be made in two places.
findings.locality_impact: Drift between the two copies produces inconsistent output for the same data.
findings.why_weakens_submission: Duplicate presentation logic is a simplification deduction; it is subsumed by the render extraction in F-006.
findings.minimal_correction_path: When extracting render helpers for F-006 (next loop), fold the two duplicated blocks into single module-level format helpers taking a BoardResult and the date-stats map.

simplification_check.structurally_necessary: Extracting _evaluate_filters passes the Shallow-module test: a small Interface (fields + thresholds in, (meets, failures) out) with real branching behind it, reached directly by tests. The pure decision is separated from the side-effecting skipped_buckets attribution, which stays at the call site.
simplification_check.helpful_simplification: search_boards shrinks; the filter decision becomes fixture-testable across all branches.
simplification_check.should_not_be_done: Do not move the skipped_buckets counters into the pure function (that would re-fuse a side effect); do not extract the network date-scan; no class hierarchy.
simplification_check.tests_after_fix: test_soundboard_snag.py gains EvaluateFiltersTests (no-filter pass, views+sounds both fail, basics pass, date-only-when-basics-pass, updated_unknown/too_old, recent-enough) at the new _evaluate_filters Interface.

backlog.title: Extract pure filter evaluation from search_boards, then render/format helpers (F-006)
backlog.why_it_matters: Largest remaining structural lever; this loop does the filter half, next loop the render half.

backlog.title: Fold the duplicated date-display + skipped-breakdown render blocks (F-005)
backlog.why_it_matters: Removes drift-prone copies; folded into the F-006 render extraction next loop.

deepening_candidates.source_friction_proven: Finding F-006 — the per-board and final-results render sections are pure formatting but inlined in the function
deepening_candidates.why_shallow_or_misplaced: There is no render Interface; the formatting is inlined and duplicated.
deepening_candidates.behavior_to_move_behind_interface: BoardResult-to-string formatting for the per-board summary and the results listing, plus the shared date-display and skipped-breakdown blocks.
deepening_candidates.test_surface_after_change: test_soundboard_snag.py asserts rendered strings for representative BoardResults without network.
deepening_candidates.smallest_first_step: Extract the date-display block into _format_updated_line(board, stats) and call it from both sites (resolves F-005).
deepening_candidates.what_not_to_do: Do not introduce a renderer class hierarchy or a templating layer; small pure functions are the honest shape.

builder_notes.pattern: Separate the pure decision from its side effect when extracting
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: A block computes a boolean/result AND mutates a counter or accumulator in the same branches.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Return the decision (and structured failure info) from a pure function; keep the mutation at the call site, driven by the returned info.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: _evaluate_filters returns (meets, [(bucket_key, reason), ...]); the loop increments skipped_buckets[bucket_key] itself.

builder_notes.pattern: Preserve a subtle ordering rule explicitly in the extracted function
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: The original had an implicit dependency (e.g., 'only check the date filter if the basic filters passed').
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Encode the dependency directly (here: evaluate the date branch only when there are no prior failures) and write a test that pins it.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: a test asserts that when views fails, the date filter is not even evaluated.

builder_notes.pattern: Chip a large finding across loops with shrinking, named evidence
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: One finding is too big for a single honest loop (filter + date-scan + render).
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Resolve one slice per loop, mark the finding carried_forward, and re-cite the residual evidence so the next loop's target is unambiguous.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: F-006 evidence narrows from 'filter+render fused' to 'results render still inlined (1339-1395)'.

narrative: Place — a good app, decomposing steadily and honestly. This loop extracts the filter decision into a pure, fully-branch-tested _evaluate_filters, separating it cleanly from the skipped_buckets side effect. F-006 is now half done; the remaining slice is the terminal rendering (which also absorbs the duplicated blocks of F-005). Ownership and concurrency stay trustworthy. The discipline to resist over-typing a 2-tuple and to keep the side effect at the call site is exactly the anti-overengineering the rubric rewards.

loop_result.what_changed: Extracted a pure _evaluate_filters(views_int, sound_count, approx_updated, min_views, min_sounds, recent_threshold, recent_days) from search_boards' inline filter block; it returns (meets, failures) where each failure is a (bucket_key, reason) tuple. search_boards now calls it, builds filter_reasons from the failures, and keeps the skipped_buckets attribution (gated on has_downloads) at the call site. Added EvaluateFiltersTests (6 tests) covering every branch including the date-only-when-basics-pass rule.
loop_result.evidence_change_is_honest: python3 -m py_compile passes; python3 -m unittest test_soundboard_snag runs 34 tests, all OK (28 prior + 6 new); --help exits 0; the extraction reproduces the original branch logic and side-effect attribution exactly (views and sounds can both fail and increment both buckets; the date branch is only evaluated when basics pass); grep confirms the inline filter logic (meets_filters = True / filter_reasons.append) no longer appears in search_boards; the function shrank to ~678 lines.

implementation_review.reason: The filter extraction is real, behavior-preserving, and introduces no regressions — all three checks pass.
